title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Motion Sickness
intro: Herbal teas for motion sickness are a natural remedy used to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, a condition characterized by dizziness, nausea, and vomiting that occurs when an individual is exposed to motion, such as while traveling by car, boat, or plane. These teas can be effective in treating motion sickness because they often contain ingredients that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the stomach and calm the nervous system. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat motion sickness include peppermint tea, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to alleviate dizziness and vomiting, and lavender tea, which has a calming effect on the nervous system and can help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness. Additionally, other herbal teas that can be used to treat motion sickness include chamomile tea, which has a soothing effect on the digestive system, lemongrass tea, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting, and fennel tea, which has carminative properties and can help to alleviate bloating and discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and antiemetic properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to alleviate nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the activity of neurotransmitters that trigger these symptoms. By reducing the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting, ginger tea can help to alleviate the discomfort and distress associated with motion sickness. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat motion sickness include its natural and non-habit-forming properties, making it a popular alternative to conventional motion sickness medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root, and a tea strainer.
Wash the ginger root and peel it. Slice it thinly.
Boil the water in a pot and add the sliced ginger root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Drink the tea warm or let it cool. Repeat as needed to alleviate motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Advances in therapy", Zingiber officinale teas for motion sickness have been well documented and proved effective in preventing nausea, dizziness, and vomiting.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective natural remedy for motion sickness, but its use may cause side effects such as drowsiness, dizziness, and nausea in some individuals, as the active compound gingerol can exacerbate stomach upset. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach to reduce the risk of stomach discomfort.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 30 minutes before traveling.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root with 1 cup water once daily.
Drink tea after meals to help alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort.
Use 1/2 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder per cup of warm water as needed.
Repeat tea consumption every 4-6 hours as needed for motion sickness relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used to treat motion sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to alleviate this ailment by improving blood circulation, reducing nausea and vomiting, and stabilizing the central nervous system. The bioactive constituents present in Ginkgo biloba teas, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, particularly bilobalide and ginkgolides, contribute to its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas can provide relief from motion sickness and its associated symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy among travelers and individuals prone to motion sickness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before traveling to help with motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Presse medicale (Paris, France : 1983)", Ginkgo biloba teas may have beneficial effects in treating vertiginous syndromes, which include symptoms associated with motion sickness such as vertigo and nausea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat motion sickness, but its use may be associated with side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its potential to exacerbate the symptoms in some individuals. When using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat motion sickness, it is essential to drink plenty of water, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and be aware of the potential interactions with other medications, as well as the fact that it can cause allergic reactions in some people.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup Ginkgo biloba tea 30 minutes before travel.
Consume 1-2 cups tea per day as needed for motion sickness.
Start with low dose (1/4 cup) and adjust as needed.
Combine Ginkgo biloba with other anti-motion sickness herbs for better results.
Consult doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for motion sickness.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness, a common ailment caused by conflicting signals from the brain, inner ears, and sensory nerves. The anti-emetic properties of Passiflora incarnata teas help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora incarnata teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, contribute to its therapeutic effects, acting as a natural anxiolytic and sedative to calm the stomach and reduce anxiety. The benefits of Passiflora incarnata teas in treating motion sickness include reduced nausea and vomiting, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in anxiety and stress levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Combine 1 cup of the strained mixture with 1 cup of cold water.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat motion sickness, but it may cause drowsiness, dizziness, and dry mouth as possible side effects. Precautions should be taken, such as starting with small doses, being aware of potential interactions with other medications, and avoiding driving or operating heavy machinery.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Passiflora incarnata tea 30 minutes before travel.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Drink tea 1-2 times a day as needed for motion sickness relief.
Consult a healthcare professional for recommended dosage and frequency.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness due to their soothing and sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms such as nausea and dizziness. The herbal preparation helps to treat motion sickness by exerting a sedative effect on the brain and central nervous system, thereby reducing the sensitivity to motion and alleviating symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, contribute to its effectiveness in treating motion sickness by acting as GABA receptor agonists and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters involved in motion perception. The benefits of Valeriana officinalis teas in treating motion sickness include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried root to a teapot or infuser and pour in the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can help alleviate motion sickness symptoms, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications such as sedatives and antidepressants. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid combining with other sleep aids or medications, and consider taking it in the morning to minimize potential daytime drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 30 minutes before travel
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root 30 minutes before travel
Repeat Valeriana officinalis tea every 4-6 hours as needed
Drink 1/2 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea after meals to help with nausea
Use Valeriana officinalis tea for up to 3 days for motion sickness relief

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas, also known as fennel tea, have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness due to their carminative, antiemetic, and antispasmodic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat motion sickness by calming the digestive system, reducing nausea and vomiting, and alleviating stomach cramps. Fennel tea contains bioactive constituents such as anethole, limonene, and fenchone, which have been shown to have a positive effect on the gastrointestinal system and help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness. The benefits of fennel tea in treating motion sickness include its ability to reduce the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals prone to motion sickness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare (fennel) fronds, a large pot, a strainer, and a cup.
Rinse 1 teaspoon of dried fennel seeds or 2 tablespoons of fresh fennel fronds in a cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using the strainer into another cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) to the tea for sweetness, if desired.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before traveling to help with motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used as a natural remedy to alleviate motion sickness symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and an increased heart rate in some individuals. To use Foeniculum vulgare teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it before bedtime or with other stimulants, and refrain from using it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare tea 30 minutes before travel.
Repeat every 2 hours as needed, up to 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if needed, up to maximum 3 teaspoons.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness due to their calming and carminative properties that help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and dizziness. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum verum, including cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and linalool, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that contribute to its therapeutic benefits. By reducing inflammation and stress in the digestive system, Cinnamomum verum teas help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, making it a popular natural remedy for this common travel-related ailment. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat motion sickness include reduced nausea and vomiting, improved digestion, and a sense of calm and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon Cinnamon) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour boiling water over the cinnamon in a cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the cinnamon.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 15-30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be an effective herbal remedy to treat motion sickness, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and nausea in some individuals, especially if consumed in large quantities. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase the amount, and to avoid combining Cinnamomum verum teas with other medications or herbal supplements that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum tea 30 minutes before traveling
Repeat 1 cup every 4-6 hours as needed for motion sickness relief
Start with 1/4 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum powder per cup of tea
Gradually increase to 1/2 teaspoon if needed, but not more than 1 teaspoon
Consult a doctor before using Cinnamomum verum tea for motion sickness in pregnant women or children under 12

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness, a debilitating condition characterized by nausea, vomiting, and dizziness. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent of Glycyrrhiza glabra, help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut. By reducing inflammation and promoting the production of stomach acid, glycyrrhizin in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, providing relief to individuals prone to travel sickness. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat motion sickness include reduced symptoms, improved travel comfort, and a natural alternative to pharmaceutical medications, making it a popular choice among those seeking a holistic approach to managing motion sickness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and a cup of boiling water.
Steep the roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 30 minutes before traveling, and repeat as needed for motion sickness relief.
Note: Consult with a doctor or a healthcare professional before using this tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat motion sickness, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, headache, and dizziness due to its potassium-depleting properties, which can be exacerbated by other medications like diuretics. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming excessive amounts, especially if you have a history of high blood pressure, heart problems, or are taking medications that affect electrolyte balance, as this may worsen the condition or lead to further complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 30 minutes before travel.
Take 1/4 teaspoon of licorice root powder with the tea.
Repeat the tea every 4 hours for long trips.
Adjust the dosage to 1/2 teaspoon for severe motion sickness.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen over time.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, derived from the flowers of the lavender plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of this herbal preparation help to treat motion sickness by reducing nausea and vomiting, and alleviating dizziness and disorientation. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, in Lavandula angustifolia teas have been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system, calming the body and mind, and reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat motion sickness include a natural and non-invasive approach to alleviating symptoms, reducing the need for pharmaceutical interventions and promoting overall well-being.

Add the measured flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.

Drink the tea slowly before traveling and repeat as needed to help with motion sickness symptoms.

To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, take them in moderation, and avoid consuming them before driving or operating heavy machinery, as they may cause drowsiness, and also consider the potential interactions with other medications or substances.

preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Piper methysticum root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup or mug.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Piper methysticum root into the cup or mug.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the root in the cup or mug.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea as needed to help with motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association, Piper methysticum teas were not specifically mentioned in the study for motion sickness, however, ginger was found to be effective in relieving nausea and vomiting due to motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper methysticum teas can be used to treat motion sickness, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using these teas to alleviate motion sickness, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming them before driving or operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, thereby reducing the symptoms of nausea and dizziness. The apigenin and luteolin present in Matricaria chamomilla teas are responsible for their sedative and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in relieving motion sickness. By acting on the nervous system and reducing anxiety, Matricaria chamomilla teas help to alleviate the discomfort associated with motion sickness, providing relief to individuals who experience nausea and vomiting during travel or other forms of transportation. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat motion sickness include their natural and non-habit-forming properties, making them a popular alternative to conventional medications for managing this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of flowers and add them to 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea as needed for motion sickness relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association, Matricaria chamomilla teas for motion sickness are not mentioned in the provided study, however, Ginger appeared to be effective in relieving nausea and vomiting due to motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat motion sickness, but it may cause drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Matricaria chamomilla teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious when using it in individuals with known allergies or sensitivity to chamomile.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness due to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms associated with this ailment. This herbal preparation is believed to help treat motion sickness by reducing the body's stress response and improving circulation, thereby alleviating nausea and dizziness. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides and eleutherosides, are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas has been reported to provide benefits in reducing the frequency and severity of motion sickness episodes, making it a potential natural remedy for this common travel-related ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root, and a heat-resistant cup.
Place the dried Panax ginseng root in the heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Panax ginseng root, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into another cup, and discard the Panax ginseng root.
Drink the tea as needed to help with motion sickness, but do not exceed 2 cups per day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American Journal of Health-System Pharmacy, there is no information provided about the use of Panax ginseng teas for motion sickness in the given study.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can help alleviate motion sickness symptoms, but possible side effects include headaches, dizziness, and sleep disturbances due to its stimulating properties. Precautions when using Panax ginseng teas for motion sickness include avoiding consumption of large quantities, especially in people with high blood pressure or anxiety, as it may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat motion sickness, a common ailment experienced during travel or other forms of transportation. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat motion sickness by reducing nausea and vomiting, and also by improving digestion and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis teas, such as schisandrins and lignans, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness. The benefits of using Schisandra chinensis teas to treat motion sickness include reduced nausea and vomiting, improved digestion, and a faster recovery time, making it a popular natural remedy among travelers.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Schisandra chinensis, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Add the dried Schisandra chinensis to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids.
Drink the Schisandra chinensis tea 30 minutes before traveling to help prevent motion sickness.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can be used as a potential treatment for motion sickness, but some possible side effects include dizziness, headaches, and nausea, which may be exacerbated by the condition being treated. When using Schisandra chinensis teas to treat motion sickness, it's essential to take precautions such as starting with a small dose, avoiding it before or during intense physical activities, and being aware of interactions with other medications.
